MOTOR.vibration_HIGH.title: Motor M-04 bearing degradation suspected
MOTOR.vibration_HIGH.what_happened: Motor vibration exceeded normal range at {first_signal_time}. Temperature and current deviated within expected causal windows. Pattern is consistent with bearing wear or mechanical imbalance.
MOTOR.vibration_HIGH.projected_consequence: If vibration continues rising, thermal protection may trip the motor. Estimated {time_to_limit} before HIHI threshold.
check_first: Inspect motor bearing — check for noise or heat on housing
Check VFD output frequency — rule out electrical imbalance
Verify vibration sensor mounting (false read if loose)
counter_evidence: Vibration sensor may give false high if mounting is loose
A VFD frequency transient can cause momentary current rise without mechanical cause
MOTOR.vibration_HIGH.authority: No automatic action taken. Operator confirmation required before any response.

MOTOR.temperature_HIGH.title: Motor M-04 thermal excursion
MOTOR.temperature_HIGH.what_happened: Motor temperature exceeded normal range at {first_signal_time}. Downstream current and speed deviations may follow within causal windows.
MOTOR.temperature_HIGH.projected_consequence: Thermal protection may trip if temperature continues rising. Estimated {time_to_limit} before HIHI threshold.
check_first: Verify motor cooling airflow and ambient temperature
Check load on shaft — mechanical drag increases heat
Confirm temperature sensor contact and calibration
counter_evidence: Ambient temperature spike can raise body temperature without mechanical fault
Sensor contact loss can read artificially high
MOTOR.temperature_HIGH.authority: No automatic action taken. Operator confirmation required before any response.

VFD.current_HIGH.title: VFD output current elevated
VFD.current_HIGH.what_happened: VFD output current exceeded limit at {first_signal_time}. Motor speed may degrade if load continues.
VFD.current_HIGH.projected_consequence: Sustained overcurrent may trip VFD protection. Estimated {time_to_limit} before trip.
check_first: Check motor mechanical load and coupling alignment
Verify VFD parameter settings and acceleration ramp
Inspect DC bus voltage — sag can cause current rise
counter_evidence: Brief inrush during acceleration is normal
Bus voltage transient can cause momentary current spike
VFD.current_HIGH.authority: No automatic action taken. Operator confirmation required before any response.

DCBUS.voltage_LOW.title: DC bus voltage sag
DCBUS.voltage_LOW.what_happened: DC bus voltage dropped below normal at {first_signal_time}. Downstream loads may be affected.
DCBUS.voltage_LOW.projected_consequence: Continued sag may cause inverter undervoltage trips. Estimated {time_to_limit} before critical threshold.
check_first: Check upstream generation — PV and mains charger output
Verify battery state of charge and health
Inspect motor branch load — mechanical overload pulls bus down
counter_evidence: Transient sag during motor start is expected
Metering lag can make bus appear low while recovering
DCBUS.voltage_LOW.authority: No automatic action taken. Operator confirmation required before any response.
